constituents: Gaiadendron ligustrinum, also known as the maidenhair tree, contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds as its active constituents. These compounds are found in various parts of the plant, including its leaves, bark, and roots. The flavonoids present in Gaiadendron ligustrinum may exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The alkaloids present in the plant may have potential therapeutic effects, such as anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities.
